issues.evidence_anchor: SPRiF shares this separation objective but further constrains neuron-level memory to a stable three-dimensional system with one real mode and one rotational pair, while confining a learned two-coordinate projective reset to the fast state.
issues.summary: The paper does not establish the incremental value of SPRiF against the closest memory/discharge or memory/reset decoupling methods in a controlled comparison.
issues.references: LSTM-LIF and the cited modular reset-decoupling method
issues.notes: Recall audit: controlled closest-prior evidence or an author-approved narrowing of the novelty spine is required.

issues.evidence_anchor: Unlike conventional multi-timescale models, whose parallel channels differ in decay but remain coupled to readout and reset, SPRiF separates state functions.
issues.summary: The introductory contrast may overgeneralize multi-timescale models and create a misleading novelty premise.
issues.notes: Recall-confirmed drop at major level: Related Work acknowledges the exceptions; retained separately as minor wording issue I-09.

issues.evidence_anchor: Because the published systems use task-specific architectures and training protocols, the table provides descriptive cross-paper context rather than a matched significance or efficiency comparison.
issues.summary: The unmatched cross-paper table cannot establish controlled superiority or efficiency.
issues.notes: Recall-confirmed drop: the manuscript consistently scopes the comparison as descriptive and unmatched.

issues.evidence_anchor: The merged variant jointly collapses the two state roles and reduces total state dimensionality, so this comparison does not isolate separation alone.
issues.summary: The central merged-state ablation confounds functional separation with a reduction from five to three state dimensions.
issues.notes: Requires a capacity-matched separation ablation or an author decision to further narrow the central mechanism claim.

issues.evidence_anchor: Mechanism ablations (accuracy, \%). The merged variant changes both state roles and total state dimensionality; it has the largest observed accuracy decrease among the tested variants on all three datasets.
issues.summary: The ablation table reports point estimates without seed counts or uncertainty, leaving small reset-direction effects statistically unresolved.
issues.notes: Requires existing seed-level results to be reported or new reruns/statistics.

issues.evidence_anchor: At each intervention timestep, the membrane coordinates of 10\% of hidden neurons were placed above threshold before spike computation, so the standard reset mechanism was applied.
issues.summary: SI-DMS overwrites functionally different coordinates across models and therefore does not isolate reset from intervention-induced state overwrite.
issues.notes: Requires intervention redesign or a materially narrower author-approved interpretation.

issues.evidence_anchor: The merged variant remained near chance (51.7\% clean and 50.4\% under maximum stress), as did LIF and ASRNN, so their clean-to-stress differences are interpreted only within that performance range.
issues.summary: SI-DMS lacks a high-performing coupled-state control, so reset-stress robustness is confounded with clean-task learnability.
issues.notes: Requires a high-performing coupled-state control selected and evaluated by the authors.

issues.evidence_anchor: SPRiF full reached 92.8\% clean accuracy and lost 0.7 percentage points, while SPRiF $\lambda{=}0$ showed a comparable 0.6-point drop.
issues.summary: The dedicated stress result does not show a benefit from learned projective reset, while benchmark effects are small and lack uncertainty.
issues.notes: Requires stronger utility evidence or an author-approved reduction in title/method prominence.

issues.evidence_anchor: Unlike conventional multi-timescale models, whose parallel channels differ in decay but remain coupled to readout and reset, SPRiF separates state functions.
issues.summary: Narrow the introductory contrast to many single-state or parallel-rate models so it matches the exceptions acknowledged in Related Work.
issues.notes: Review-mode checklist item; no manuscript edit applied without author sign-off.

issues.section: Fast Discharge Dynamics and Projective Reset
issues.evidence_anchor: The learned coefficients $\eta_0,\eta_1\in(0,1)$ are fast leaks, $\kappa$ couples the second fast coordinate to the first, and $G\in\mathbb{R}^{2\times3}$ projects slow dynamics into discharge space.
issues.summary: The manuscript calls the discharge state fast without stating any learned timescale ordering relative to the slow spectral factors.
issues.notes: Review-mode checklist item; no manuscript edit applied without author sign-off.

issues.evidence_anchor: Because the slow state is computed before spikes and absent from the reset equation, its dynamics remain separate from post-spike correction.
issues.summary: This sentence is broader than the same-timestep direct-local-reset guarantee because recurrent spikes can affect the next slow update.
issues.notes: Review-mode checklist item; no manuscript edit applied without author sign-off.

issues.evidence_anchor: With no subsequent input, this diagnostic isolates the temporal basis $A^kB$ in Eq.~\eqref{eq:spectral_impulse_response} from task-specific input activity and subsequent post-spike correction.
issues.summary: The impulse response characterizes the isolated slow transition rather than the effective input-to-spike kernel of the full neuron.
issues.notes: Review-mode checklist item; no manuscript edit applied without author sign-off.

issues.evidence_anchor: On GSC and QTDB, selected slow-decay SPRiF real modes have derived time constants of approximately 272 and 138 steps, respectively, compared with 34 and 46 steps for the available trained ASRNN membrane kernels.
issues.summary: The time-constant comparison is selection- and checkpoint-limited and is not a like-for-like population comparison.
issues.notes: Review-mode checklist item; no manuscript edit applied without author sign-off.

issues.summary: Explain why PS-MNIST full accuracy is 95.82 in the ablation table but 95.86 plus/minus 0.14 in the main table.
issues.notes: Review-mode checklist item; no manuscript edit applied without author sign-off.

issues.evidence_anchor: Its reported end-to-end parameter count is lower than that of the highest-accuracy listed baseline on four datasets and comparable on QTDB.
issues.summary: Parameter-count statements need an explicit reminder that cross-paper architecture and counting conventions are unmatched.
issues.notes: Review-mode checklist item; no manuscript edit applied without author sign-off.

issues.evidence_anchor: Table~\ref{tab:si_dms} reports clean ($K=0$) and maximum-stress ($K=8$) accuracy averaged over three seeds.
issues.summary: The SI-DMS table omits dispersion for the reported three-seed averages, although the figure caption states that curves show seed standard deviations.
issues.notes: Review-mode checklist item; no manuscript edit applied without author sign-off.
